subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of mean as a measure of central tendency
Understand how the mean behaves when values are added or removed
Learn the concept of median in a dataset
Calculate mean and median using frequency tables
Use spreadsheets to calculate totals and averages
Interpret data using dot plots and line graphs
Analyse real-life data and identify trends

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Mean
topics_covered: Revision of mean from earlier grades
Mean as the center of data
student_activities: Calculate mean of pairs of numbers
Plot numbers and mark the mean on a number line

lesson_plan.title: Mean as a Balancing Point
teaching_strategy: Guided explanation of distance balancing concept
student_activities: Verify that total distances on both sides of the mean are equal
Discuss whether more than one center is possible

lesson_plan.title: Effect of Adding or Removing Values
topics_covered: How mean changes when a new value is added
student_activities: Add values greater than the mean and observe changes
Experiment with removing values from datasets

lesson_plan.title: Median and Its Behaviour
topics_covered: Effect of adding values on median
student_activities: Observe how adding values changes the median

lesson_plan.title: Mean and Median with Frequencies
student_activities: Create a frequency table from sample data

lesson_plan.title: Using Spreadsheets for Data Analysis
topics_covered: Using formulas such as SUM and AVERAGE
student_activities: Calculate totals and averages using formulas

lesson_plan.title: Line Graphs and Data Interpretation
student_activities: Draw a line graph from temperature or rainfall data

teacher_tip: Use visual tools like dot plots and line graphs frequently so students clearly understand mean and median as central values in data.
